2605.20740.title: Distribution-Aware Reward: Reinforcement Learning over Predictive Distributions for LLM Regression
2605.20740.paper_summary: Large language models can predict real-valued quantities from heterogeneous inputs such as text, code, and molecular strings, but most training objectives score each decoded floating-point number independently, improving point estimates without ensuring calibrated predictive distributions. This limits applications requiring candidate ranking or uncertainty estimation. We introduce Distribution-Aware Reward, an on-policy reinforcement learning objective whose main contribution is to train language models to produce better predictive distributions for regression tasks, rather than only optimizing individual decoded outputs against scalar targets. Our method treats multiple decoded samples as an empirical predictive distribution, evaluates it with the Continuous Ranked Probability Score, and assigns leave-one-out credit based on each rollout's marginal contribution to distribution quality, rewarding predictions that are both accurate and appropriately dispersed. We evaluate our method on a controlled Gaussian-mixture task, code performance prediction, and molecular property prediction from SMILES strings. Across tasks, our method improves over supervised fine-tuning and pointwise reinforcement learning baselines, with strong rank-correlation gains, including a 6-point Spearman improvement on KBSS. On MoleculeNet, it uses only SMILES strings yet remains competitive with strong graph-based and 3D molecular models. Further analyses show that our method mitigates rollout diversity collapse and improves uncertainty diagnostics, suggesting that directly optimizing predictive distributions makes language model regression more robust and better calibrated.
2605.20740.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型可以從異質輸入（如文本、代碼和分子字符串）中預測實數值量，但大多數訓練目標獨立評分每個解碼的浮點數，改善點估計卻不確保校準的預測分佈。這限制了需要候選排名或不確定性估計的應用。我們引入了分佈感知獎勵，這是一種政策內強化學習目標，其主要貢獻是訓練語言模型產生更好的回歸任務預測分佈，而不僅僅是針對標量目標優化單個解碼輸出。我們的方法將多個解碼樣本視為經驗預測分佈，使用連續排名概率分數進行評估，並根據每次回合對分佈質量的邊際貢獻分配留一法信用，獎勵既準確又適當分散的預測。我們在受控的高斯混合任務、代碼性能預測和從SMILES字符串中預測分子性質上評估我們的方法。在各項任務中，我們的方法優於監督微調和逐點強化學習基線，並且具有強大的排名相關性增益，包括在KBSS上提高6點的斯皮爾曼相關性。在MoleculeNet上，它僅使用SMILES字符串，但仍然與強大的基於圖形和3D分子模型競爭。進一步的分析顯示，我們的方法減輕了回合多樣性的崩潰並改善了不確定性診斷，這表明直接優化預測分佈使語言模型回歸更加穩健且更好地校準。
2605.20740.author: Jungsoo Park et.al.
2605.20740.authors: Jungsoo Park, Hyungjoo Chae, Ethan Mendes, Jay DeYoung, Varsha Kishore, Wei Xu, Alan Ritter
